{
  "assessment": {
    "meta": {
      "assessmentType": "Session assessment",
      "totalMarks": 20,
      "totalQuestions": 10,
      "durationMinutes": 15,
      "language": "English",
      "preferredDifficulty": "Balanced",
      "paperObjective": "Assess only the taught session content with the requested paper pattern.",

      ]
    },
    "sessionAnalysis": {
      "assessedLearningOutcomes": [
        "Define a polynomial and identify valid vs. invalid expressions.",
        "Determine the degree of any given polynomial.",
        "Classify polynomials as monomial, binomial, or trinomial.",
        "Classify polynomials as linear, quadratic, or cubic based on degree."
      ],
      "assessedConcepts": [
        "Polynomial Definition",
        "Degree of a Polynomial",
        "Classification by Terms",
        "Classification by Degree"
      ],
      "conceptCoveragePriorities": [
        "Identifying non-polynomials (negative/fractional exponents)",
        "Determining degree for classification"
      ],
      "misconceptionTargets": [
        "Confusing variables with constants",
        "Assuming any algebraic expression is a polynomial regardless of exponent"
      ],
      "competencyFocus": [
        "Apply",
        "Analyze"
      ]
    },
    "blueprint": {
      "sectionPlan": [
        {
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "title": "Unit II: Algebra",

        {
          "outcome": "Define a polynomial and identify valid vs. invalid expressions.",
          "questionRefs": [

          "questionRefs": [

        "easy": 30,

      },

        "application": 30,

        "evaluation": 0
      },
      "competencyDistribution": {
        "understanding": 4,
        "application": 4,

      },
      "questionDistribution": {
        "mcq": 3,
        "veryShortAnswer": 3,
        "shortAnswer": 2,
        "longAnswer": 2
      },
      "timeAllocation": [
        {
          "section": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "minutes": 15
        }
      ]
    },
    "paper": {
      "instructions": [
        "All questions are compulsory.",
        "Answer all questions using only the concepts taught in this session."
      ],
      "sections": [
        {
          "id": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "title": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "marks": 20,
          "questionCount": 10,
          "questionRefs": [
            "q1",
            "q2",
            "q3",
            "q4",
            "q5",
            "q6",
            "q7",
            "q8",
            "q9",
            "q10"
          ],
          "source": "assessment_framework"
        }
      ],
      "questions": [
        {
          "id": "q1",
          "questionNumber": 1,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "Which of the following expressions is NOT a polynomial?",
          "options": [
            "A. 3x^2 + 5x - 2",
            "B. x + 1/x",
            "C. 7",
            "D. x^3 - 1"
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Polynomial Definition"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Valid vs Invalid Expressions"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "analysis",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Detailed content: If we see x^(-2) or sqrt(x), it is NOT a polynomial."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q2",
          "questionNumber": 2,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "What is the degree of the polynomial P(x) = 5x^3 - 2x + 1?",
          "options": [
            "A. 1",
            "B. 2",
            "C. 3",
            "D. 0"
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO2"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Degree of a Polynomial"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Determining Degree"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "understanding",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Identify the degree of 5x^3 - 2x + 1."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q3",
          "questionNumber": 3,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "A polynomial containing exactly three terms is called a:",
          "options": [
            "A. Monomial",
            "B. Binomial",
            "C. Trinomial",
            "D. Linear Polynomial"
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO3"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Classification by Terms"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Naming Polynomials"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "recall",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Detailed content: A polynomial with three terms is a Trinomial."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q4",
          "questionNumber": 4,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "veryShortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Is the expression sqrt(x) + 5 a polynomial? Give one reason for your answer.",
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "One sentence",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Polynomial Definition"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Valid vs Invalid Expressions"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "analysis",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Detailed content: If we see sqrt(x), it is NOT a polynomial."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q5",
          "questionNumber": 5,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "veryShortAnswer",
          "prompt": "What is the degree of a non-zero constant polynomial (e.g., P(x) = 7)?",
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "One word/number",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO2"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Degree of a Polynomial"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Determining Degree"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "understanding",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Lesson Block: What is the degree of 7x^0? Expected answer: Degree is 0."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q6",
          "questionNumber": 6,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "veryShortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Classify the polynomial '4x^2' based on the number of terms.",
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "One word",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO3"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Classification by Terms"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Naming Polynomials"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Detailed content: A polynomial with one term is a Monomial (e.g., 4x^2)."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q7",
          "questionNumber": 7,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Identify the coefficient of x and the constant term in the expression 3x^2 - 5x + 8.",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "Two points",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Polynomial Definition"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Components of a Polynomial"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Guided Practice: Ask students to identify the coefficient of x in 5x^2 - 3x + 1."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q8",
          "questionNumber": 8,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Classify the polynomial x^2 + 4 in terms of both its degree and number of terms.",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "Two points",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO3",
            "LO4"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Classification by Terms",
            "Classification by Degree"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Naming Polynomials"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Classify x^2 + 4 as linear, quadratic, or cubic."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q9",
          "questionNumber": 9,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "longAnswer",
          "subtype": "longAnswer",
          "prompt": "Consider the following expressions: (i) 2x^3 + x - 5, (ii) 4x^2 - 1/x, (iii) y^2 + 2y + 1. \n\na) Identify which of these are polynomials and why.\n\nb) For those that are polynomials, state their degree.",
          "marks": 5,
          "expectedLength": "Detailed explanation",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO1",
            "LO2"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Polynomial Definition",
            "Degree of a Polynomial"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Valid vs Invalid Expressions",
            "Determining Degree"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "analysis",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "hard",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Detailed content: Variables must have whole number exponents. If we see 1/x, it is NOT a polynomial."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q10",
          "questionNumber": 10,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
          "type": "longAnswer",
          "subtype": "longAnswer",
          "prompt": "Create three different polynomials that satisfy the following conditions:\n1. A Quadratic Binomial\n2. A Cubic Trinomial\n3. A Linear Monomial\nFor each, explain how it meets both the degree and term requirements.",
          "marks": 5,
          "expectedLength": "Detailed explanation",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "LO3",
            "LO4"
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Classification by Terms",
            "Classification by Degree"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Naming Polynomials"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "hard",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Guided Practice: Have students write one linear and one quadratic polynomial."
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "evaluation": {
      "answerKey": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q1",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "B. x + 1/x",
            "explanation": "A polynomial cannot have a variable in the denominator (which implies a negative exponent, x^-1).",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q2",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "C. 3",
            "explanation": "The highest power of the variable x is 3.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q3",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "C. Trinomial",
            "explanation": "By definition, a polynomial with three terms is a trinomial.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q4",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "No. Because the variable x is under a square root, which means it has a fractional exponent (1/2), and polynomials must have non-negative integer exponents.",
            "explanation": "Polynomials require whole number exponents.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "veryShortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q5",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "0",
            "explanation": "A constant can be written as 7x^0, so the degree is 0.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "veryShortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q6",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "Monomial",
            "explanation": "It consists of only one term.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "veryShortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q7",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "Coefficient of x: -5; Constant term: 8",
            "explanation": "The number multiplying the variable x is -5, and the fixed number is 8.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q8",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "Degree: Quadratic (degree 2); Terms: Binomial (2 terms)",
            "explanation": "Highest power is 2, and there are two distinct terms.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "(i) Polynomial; Degree 3. (ii) Not a polynomial because of the term 1/x (negative exponent). (iii) Polynomial; Degree 2.",
            "explanation": "Check for whole number exponents and identify highest power.",
            "marks": 5,
            "subtype": "longAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q10",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-ii-algebra",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit II: Algebra",
            "answer": "Example answers: 1. x^2 + 5 (Degree 2, 2 terms). 2. x^3 + x^2 + 1 (Degree 3, 3 terms). 3. 4x (Degree 1, 1 term).",
            "explanation": "Student must provide valid expressions and correctly justify the degree and number of terms.",
            "marks": 5,
            "subtype": "longAnswer"
          }
        ]
      },
      "markingScheme": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q1",
            "totalMarks": 1,
            "markBreakdown": [
              {
                "criterion": "Correct option selected",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "awardGuidance": [
              "Award full marks only for Option B."
            ]
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q4",
            "totalMarks": 1,
            "markBreakdown": [
              {
                "criterion": "Correct 'No' answer",
                "marks": 0.5
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Valid reason regarding exponents/sqrt",
                "marks": 0.5
              }
            ],
            "awardGuidance": [
              "Must mention that sqrt(x) is not a whole number exponent."
            ]
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q7",
            "totalMarks": 2,
            "markBreakdown": [
              {
                "criterion": "Correct coefficient of x (-5)",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Correct constant term (8)",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "awardGuidance": [
              "Ensure the negative sign is included for the coefficient."
            ]
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "totalMarks": 5,
            "markBreakdown": [
              {
                "criterion": "Correct identification of (i) and its degree",
                "marks": 2
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Correct identification of (ii) as non-polynomial with reason",
                "marks": 2
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Correct identification of (iii) and its degree",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "awardGuidance": [
              "Deduct marks if the reason for (ii) is missing."
            ]
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q10",
            "totalMarks": 5,
            "markBreakdown": [
              {
                "criterion": "Correct Quadratic Binomial + Explanation",
                "marks": 2
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Correct Cubic Trinomial + Explanation",
                "marks": 2
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Correct Linear Monomial + Explanation",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "awardGuidance": [
              "Award marks if the expression matches the name and explanation is logically sound."
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      "rubrics": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q10",
            "totalMarks": 5,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "Accuracy of expression (matches degree and term count)",
                "marks": 3
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Clarity of explanation/justification",
                "marks": 2
              }
            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "1. x^2 + 1: Degree is 2 (Quadratic) and it has 2 terms (Binomial). 2. x^3 + x + 1: Degree is 3 (Cubic) and it has 3 terms (Trinomial). 3. 5x: Degree is 1 (Linear) and it has 1 term (Monomial)."
          }
        ]
      },
      "generalInstructions": [
        "Award marks point-wise for each valid idea.",
        "Be strict about the definition of polynomials regarding exponents."
      ],
      "evaluatorInstructions": [
        "Use only the taught session content when evaluating student responses. Do not penalize for lack of advanced notation if the concept is correct."
      ],
      "moderationNotes": [
        "Maintain consistency across equivalent answers in q10 as students will create different polynomials."
      ]
    },
    "summary": {
      "coverageSummary": [
        "The paper covers all four learning outcomes: definition, degree identification, term-based classification, and degree-based classification."
      ],
      "omittedContent": [],
      "balanceNotes": [
        "The paper starts with basic recall (MCQs), moves to application (Short Answer), and ends with synthesis/analysis (Long Answer)."
      ]
    },
    "validation": {
      "exactPatternChecks": {
        "passed": true,
        "details": [
          "Question pattern matches: 3 MCQ, 3 VSA, 2 SA, 2 LA."
        ]
      },
      "marksChecks": {
        "passed": true,
        "details": [
          "Total marks = (3*1) + (3*1) + (2*2) + (2*5) = 20. Matches requested total."
        ]
      },
      "sectionChecks": {
        "passed": true,
        "details": [
          "All questions assigned to 'section-unit-ii-algebra'."
        ]
      },
      "alignmentChecks": {
        "passed": true,
        "details": [
          "Question paper, answer key, and marking scheme are aligned via IDs q1-q10."
        ]
      },
      "scopeBoundaryChecks": {
        "passed": true,
        "details": [
          "No content from future sessions or other chapters included. Only session 1 material used."
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
